stories.headline: King Charles III Addresses US Congress as State Visit Begins
stories.summary: King Charles III arrived at the White House on April 28 for a four-day state visit marking the 250th anniversary of American independence. The British monarch delivered a speech to Congress, only the second time a British monarch has addressed a joint congressional session. The visit comes amid strained UK-US relations, with tensions over Iran policy and other geopolitical issues. A formal state dinner was held in the East Room of the White House. The visit reflects efforts to strengthen the historic transatlantic relationship during a period of international uncertainty.

stories.headline: EU Approves 90 Billion Euro Ukraine Loan and 20th Sanctions Package Against Russia
stories.summary: The European Union formally approved a 90-billion-euro support loan to Ukraine and adopted its 20th comprehensive sanctions package against Russia on April 23, with final implementation on April 28. The sanctions package targets Russia's energy, banking, financial services, trade sectors and cryptocurrency networks, representing the largest package of individual listings in two years. The measures include further restrictions on Russia's shadow fleet of ageing oil tankers and expanded economic restrictions on Russian military-industrial complex. The agreement was reached after Hungary and Slovakia dropped objections once Ukraine restarted oil flows through the Druzhba pipeline. The package represents the EU's continued commitment to supporting Ukraine while punishing Russian aggression.

stories.headline: EU's New Border Management System Becomes Fully Operational Across Schengen Countries
stories.summary: The European Union's new automated border management system went fully operational across all Schengen countries on April 28, 2026. The system replaces the traditional manual passport stamping process with a fully automated registration system for non-EU nationals travelling to the EU. The new system enhances security, efficiency and convenience for travellers and border authorities. This represents a significant modernization of Schengen border procedures and strengthens the EU's external border management capabilities. The implementation reflects the EU's ongoing digital transformation efforts in security and administrative processes.

stories.headline: UK PM Starmer Faces Parliamentary Vote on Mandelson Vetting Inquiry
stories.summary: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer faced a parliamentary vote on April 28 regarding a possible inquiry into whether he misled the House of Commons over the appointment of Peter Mandelson as British ambassador to Washington. Mandelson, a controversial figure with ties to Jeffrey Epstein, reportedly failed security vetting but was appointed to the post regardless. The Conservative Party initiated the vote as Starmer's political standing weakens following a recent landslide election victory. Starmer has vowed to remain as Labour leader and fight to the end, but his popularity has significantly declined and party members are growing increasingly critical. The inquiry outcome could have serious implications for his political future.

stories.headline: European Parliament Debates Pet Protection, Antisemitism, and Budget Negotiations
stories.summary: The European Parliament plenary session from April 27-30 addressed multiple critical issues affecting EU citizens and institutions. MEPs approved new rules to protect dogs and cats by banning abusive and cruel practices. Parliament also debated antisemitism following recent attacks in the Netherlands and Belgium, calling for stronger measures. MEPs discussed digital markets enforcement, cyberbullying legislation, and the EU's long-term budget negotiations. The European Commission is expected to table new legislation establishing a common definition of rape based on freely given, informed and revocable consent. These debates reflect the Parliament's focus on consumer protection, fundamental rights and the EU's policy agenda.

stories.headline: Trump Threatens New Tariffs on European Allies Amid Greenland Tensions
stories.summary: US President Trump has threatened new tariffs on European allies as tensions escalate over Greenland amid escalating annexation threats. European nations are responding by sending additional troops to Greenland to counter the US pressure and assert their sovereignty. The situation represents a significant source of tension in transatlantic relations and threatens European economic interests through potential trade restrictions. European leaders are mobilizing to protect their strategic interests in the Arctic region. The escalating tensions underline broader divisions emerging between the US and European allies on geopolitical strategy.

stories.headline: EU LIFE 2026 Programme Hosts Info Days on Environmental Funding Priorities
stories.summary: The European Commission's EU LIFE 2026 programme hosted online info days from April 28-30, 2026, highlighting priority topics for 2026 funding calls and the LIFE sub-programmes. The programme focuses on supporting environmental and climate projects across the EU. The info days provide funding guidance for projects addressing climate change, biodiversity protection, and sustainable resource management. The LIFE programme is a key EU funding mechanism for environmental initiatives. These events represent the EU's commitment to advancing environmental sustainability goals.
